title: Max Window Average (Sliding Window O(n))
description:
Given an integer array `nums` and integer `k`, return the maximum average value of any contiguous subarray of length `k`.

**The point**: a brute-force solution recomputes the sum of every window in O(k) each time → total O(n·k). A sliding window slides one element at a time, adding the new and subtracting the old, for O(n) total.
hints: Brute force: for each starting index, sum k elements. That's O(n·k).
Notice that consecutive windows share k-1 elements. You can update the sum in O(1).
Maintain a 'window sum' variable. When you slide right by one, add nums[i] and subtract nums[i-k].
